output:
I’m locating the story changes and the relevant tests first, then I’ll inspect the implementation against correctness, security, architecture, and the stated quality scope before giving a verdict.I found the implementation footprint in `src/lib/workflow-runner.ts` and its tests. Next I’m reading the story spec and the actual diff together so I can judge AC coverage instead of just code shape.I have the spec and the diff. The implementation is small, but the quality claim depends on test depth and whether the runner’s resume validation can admit malformed snapshots, so I’m checking both the restore guard and the AC-to-test mapping now.I’ve confirmed the runner behavior and the new tests. I’m checking whether the restore guard is actually strong enough and whether the “quality” task really covered the acceptance criteria it claims, because those are the two places I expect review failures if any.I hit a bad local test invocation flag, which isn’t relevant to the review itself. I’m now validating the restore guard with a direct XState probe so I can tell whether it prevents bad snapshots or only filters the most obvious junk.The restore guard is the main correctness risk. I’m checking it against XState itself with a minimal machine, because the current predicate accepts almost anything with a `value` field and that can still blow up at actor startup.I have enough for the review. One issue is a real runtime robustness bug in the new restore guard; the others are in the “quality” deliverable itself, where the proof and tests overstate AC coverage.1. High: The new restore guard is too weak and still admits malformed snapshots. In [`src/lib/workflow-runner.ts:17`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts#L17) the predicate returns true for any object with a `value` key, and the runner then resumes from it at [`src/lib/workflow-runner.ts:145`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts#L145). A minimal XState repro with `createActor(machine, { snapshot: { value: 'a' } })` in this repo crashes during restore, so a matching-hash but malformed snapshot can still take the process down instead of warning and starting fresh. The added test only rejects `{ context: {} }` at [`src/lib/__tests__/workflow-runner.test.ts:1717`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-runner.test.ts#L1717), so this failure mode is untested.

2. Medium: The “quality” verification artifact does not match the story’s actual acceptance criteria and therefore overstates coverage. [`verification/26-2-snapshot-resume-config-hash-validation-proof.md:8`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/verification/26-2-snapshot-resume-config-hash-validation-proof.md#L8) invents/renumbers ACs (`configHash matches` as AC3, line-count limits as AC12), which shifts the real story AC numbering. It then marks AC6-AC9 as passed via inference/code inspection at [`verification/26-2-snapshot-resume-config-hash-validation-proof.md:121`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/verification/26-2-snapshot-resume-config-hash-validation-proof.md#L121) instead of direct evidence. That means the quality deliverable does not reliably prove AC coverage.

3. Medium: One of the new tests is effectively non-assertive and leaves a real AC gap. The test at [`src/lib/__tests__/workflow-runner.test.ts:1672`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-runner.test.ts#L1672) claims “does NOT call clearSnapshot when hashes match,” but it never asserts that `mismatchDiscards.length === 0`; it only checks that no warning was logged. More broadly, the only explicit “do not re-execute completed work on resume” test is the older sprint-only case at [`src/lib/__tests__/workflow-runner.test.ts:1533`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-runner.test.ts#L1533), not the general same-config resume path required by story 26-2 AC1/AC8.

<verdict>fail</verdict>
<issues>
1. Malformed matching-hash snapshots can still be treated as restorable and crash XState restore.
2. The verification proof renumbers/invents acceptance criteria and over-claims coverage.
3. The new test suite has a vacuous assertion and does not directly cover core non-reexecution resume behavior for 26-2.
</issues>
